name: Valletta
description: Malta's Baroque capital and UNESCO World Heritage city. Explore the Grand Harbour, St John's Co-Cathedral, and the Upper Barrakka Gardens.

name: Mdina
description: The Silent City — a medieval walled town perched on a hilltop. Best visited early morning before the crowds. Stunning views across the island.

name: Ħaġar Qim
description: One of the oldest free-standing structures in the world, dating to 3600–3200 BC. A UNESCO World Heritage prehistoric temple with an open-air visitor centre.

name: Fort St Angelo
description: A formidable medieval fortress in Birgu that played a pivotal role in the Great Siege of Malta in 1565. Offers panoramic views over the Grand Harbour.

name: Gozo Island
description: Malta's quieter sister island. Take the 25-minute ferry from Ċirkewwa. Visit the Citadella, Azure Window site, and swim at Ramla Bay.

name: Dingli Cliffs
description: The highest point in Malta at 253 metres above sea level. Breathtaking clifftop walks with dramatic views over the Mediterranean. Best at sunset.

name: Buskett Gardens
description: Malta's only woodland area — a rare and peaceful forested retreat in this sun-scorched island. Perfect for shaded walks on hot days.

name: Popeye Village
description: A quirky film set built for the 1980 Popeye musical at Anchor Bay. Now a family attraction with swimming and boat trips in a picturesque rocky bay.

name: Blue Lagoon
description: Malta's most famous swimming spot on the tiny island of Comino. Crystal-clear turquoise water, perfect for snorkelling. Boat trips from Ċirkewwa or St Paul's Bay.

name: Golden Bay
description: One of Malta's most popular sandy beaches on the northwest coast. Backed by gentle dunes with sunbeds, a café, and regular bus connections.

name: Mellieħa Bay
description: Malta's largest sandy beach — a wide, shallow, family-friendly bay in the north of the island. Great for swimming and watersports. Easy to reach by bus.

name: St Peter's Pool
description: A stunning natural limestone pool carved by the sea on Malta's south coast. No sand — just dramatic rocky ledges for jumping and crystal-clear deep water.

name: Marsaxlokk
description: A traditional fishing village famous for its colourful luzzu boats. Sunday market is a highlight — fresh fish, local produce, and crafts.

name: Sliema & St Julian's
description: Malta's modern seafront strip. Great for restaurants, cafés, and evening walks along the promenade. Vibrant nightlife in Paceville.
